title: fraxinus excelsior var. biloba
intro: Fraxinus excelsior var. biloba, also known as the double-lobed ash, is a variety of the common ash tree found in Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive leaves that are deeply divided into two lobes. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of the Fraxinus excelsior var. biloba have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and providing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Fraxinus excelsior var. biloba can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
